creator: avg@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Alfred Perlstein from comment #11)
> This is incorrect, you please must read the fine manual page.  This is exactly the case.  Without a .vimrc AND without a /usr/local/etc/vimrc then vim goes into compat mode

Please point me to documentation that says this.
Because my copy (which I *have* read) says:

       Vim behaves differently, depending on the name of the command (the exe-
       cutable may still be the same file).

       vim       The "normal" way, everything is default.

and also:

       -C          Compatible.  Set the 'compatible' option.  This  will  make
                   Vim  behave  mostly  like  Vi,  even  though  a .vimrc file
                   exists.

> which breaks arrow keys on many OSes, including OS-X ssh and using virtualbox.
> I have not tried vmware, but those are broken as well.

I fail to see how this is relevant at all.  I am talking about FreeBSD vim package that is installed on FreeBSD.

> I may not have explained this, but I was trying to get FreeBSD more use as desktop here at the office (even in a virtual machine) and with vim broken by default it was a complete turn-off.  

I am using FreeBSD as a desktop and vim worked pretty well out of the box for me.  I do have certain customizations in vimrc, but I never had to fix arrow keys.  Maybe there is something wrong with your terminal setup and vim fails to recognize the keys.

> Srrow keys and backspace should work inside of an editor by default because that is all they know.

No argument.

> Please retry your experiments with no vimrc and you will see the problem. 

I retried and I did not see the problems that you had.
